# Style Reference Guide

<!-- Generated by design-style-agent -->

## Typography

- **h1** — Page titles (Dashboard, Payment Management, Payments Made). Use `text-2xl font-bold` on desktop, `text-xl font-bold` on mobile.
- **h2** — Section headings (chart titles, grid titles, modal titles). Use `text-lg font-semibold`.
- **h3** — Card headers and subsection labels. Use `text-base font-semibold`.
- **Body text** — Default `text-sm` (14px) for table cells, descriptions, and form labels.
- **Caption/label** — `text-xs text-muted-foreground` for timestamps, secondary metadata, column sub-labels.
- **Numeric display** — Currency values and counts use `font-mono tabular-nums` for alignment in grids and metric cards.
- **Font weight convention** — Bold for headings and primary metrics only. Semi-bold for interactive labels (buttons, tabs). Regular for body content. Avoid light weights.

## Spacing

- **Page padding** — `p-6` on desktop, `p-4` on tablet, `p-3` on mobile.
- **Section gap** — `gap-6` between major page sections (charts row, grid section).
- **Card padding** — `p-4` for metric cards and chart containers. `p-6` for larger content cards.
- **Grid cell padding** — `px-3 py-2` for table cells. `px-4 py-3` for header cells.
- **Form field spacing** — `gap-4` between form fields. `gap-2` between label and input.
- **Button spacing** — `gap-2` between adjacent buttons in a toolbar. `gap-3` between button groups.
- **Modal padding** — `p-6` for modal content. `gap-4` between modal sections.

## Component Selection

- **Card** — Use `<Card>` for each dashboard metric, each chart container, and the grid wrapper on all screens. Cards provide visual grouping and consistent elevation.
- **Dialog** — Use `<Dialog>` for all confirmation modals (Park, Unpark, Initiate Payment, Reset Demo). Keep modals focused on a single confirmation action.
- **AlertDialog** — Use `<AlertDialog>` for destructive or irreversible confirmations (Initiate Payment, Reset Demo) to require explicit user action.
- **Table** — Use `<Table>` for the payment grids (Main Grid, Parked Grid, Payments Made). Wrap in a Card for the container.
- **Tabs** — Use `<Tabs>` on Screen 2 to switch between Main Grid and Parked Grid views. Default to Main Grid tab.
- **Badge** — Use `<Badge>` for payment Status display (READY, PARKED, PROCESSED). Color-code: default variant for READY, secondary for PARKED, outline for PROCESSED.
- **Button** — Primary variant for main actions (Initiate Payment). Secondary/outline for supporting actions (Park, Unpark). Destructive variant for Reset Demo.
- **Checkbox** — Use `<Checkbox>` in grid rows for multi-select (bulk Park/Unpark).
- **Input** — Use `<Input>` for search bars with a search icon prefix.
- **Toast** — Use `<Sonner>` (toast) for error notifications (API failures, PDF download errors) and success confirmations.
- **Skeleton** — Use `<Skeleton>` as loading placeholders while API data is being fetched.
- **DropdownMenu** — Use for user profile menu (role display, theme toggle, logout).
- **Select** — Use `<Select>` for agency filter dropdown on the Dashboard.

## Layout

- **Max content width** — `max-w-screen-2xl` (1536px) centered with `mx-auto`. Full-width sidebar layout.
- **Dashboard grid** — 3-column grid for metric cards on desktop (`grid-cols-3`), 2 columns on tablet (`md:grid-cols-2`), single column on mobile. Charts row uses `grid-cols-2` on desktop, stacked on mobile.
- **Payment grid** — Full-width table within a Card. Horizontal scroll on mobile (`overflow-x-auto`).
- **Sidebar** — Persistent sidebar on desktop (240px width). Collapsible hamburger menu on tablet/mobile. Dark navy background matching brand.
- **Responsive breakpoints** — Mobile: `< 768px`, Tablet: `768px–1279px`, Desktop: `1280px+`.
- **Agency Summary grid (Dashboard)** — Full-width table below the charts section. Each row is clickable.

## Motion & Animation

- **Transitions** — Use `transition-colors duration-150` for hover states on buttons, table rows, and sidebar items. Keep animations subtle for a professional financial application.
- **Modal enter/exit** — Use the existing `modalEnter` and `modalExit` keyframes from globals.css. 200ms duration.
- **Toast slide** — Use the existing `slideInFromRight` and `slideOutToRight` keyframes. 300ms duration.
- **Loading states** — Use Skeleton shimmer animation for data loading. No spinners except for mutation actions (Park, Unpark, Initiate Payment) where a small spinner inside the button indicates processing.
- **Reduced motion** — The existing `prefers-reduced-motion` media query in globals.css handles reduced motion globally. No additional handling needed.

## Accessibility

- **Contrast ratios** — All text meets WCAG AA minimum (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text). The navy/white and teal/navy combinations in the token file are designed for adequate contrast.
- **Focus rings** — Teal focus ring (`--ring`) provides clear visibility on both light and dark backgrounds. Do not suppress default focus indicators.
- **Keyboard navigation** — All interactive elements (buttons, checkboxes, table rows, tabs) must be keyboard-accessible. Use standard HTML semantics (button, input, a) rather than divs with click handlers.
- **Screen readers** — Use `aria-label` on icon-only buttons. Use `role="status"` on toast containers. Use proper `<th>` and `scope` attributes on table headers.
- **Color alone** — Do not convey meaning through color alone. Status badges use text labels alongside color. Chart data should include accessible labels/tooltips.

## Brand Tone

- **Microcopy style** — Professional and direct. This is a financial operations tool for real estate commission management. Avoid casual language.
- **Button labels** — Action-oriented: "Park Payment", "Unpark Payment", "Initiate Payment", "Download Invoice", "Reset Demo Data". No ambiguous labels like "Submit" or "Go".
- **Error messages** — Show the server-provided message when available. Fallback: "An unexpected error occurred. Please try again." No technical jargon (no HTTP status codes, no stack traces).
- **Empty states** — "No payments found" for empty grids. "No results match your search" when a filter returns nothing. Keep it factual, not whimsical.
- **Confirmation modals** — Clearly state the action and its scope: "You are about to process 12 payments totalling R 1 234 567,89. This action cannot be undone."
- **Success messages** — "Payment processed successfully" or "2 payments parked successfully". Include the count for bulk actions.
- **Currency formatting** — Always use `en-ZA` locale: `R 1 234 567,89` (space as thousands separator, comma as decimal). Use `Intl.NumberFormat('en-ZA', { style: 'currency', currency: 'ZAR' })`.
- **Date formatting** — Use South African format: `DD/MM/YYYY`. Use `Intl.DateTimeFormat('en-ZA')` for consistency.
